description: SSoT for the behavioral test kit. scenarios.md is generated from this via run.py --emit-md. Each rubric: pass_all = list of groups (each group needs >=1 regex match); fail_any (per scenario) + global_fail_any force a FAIL. A scenario PASSES iff every pass_all group matches AND no fail regex matches.

scenarios.title: The too-good result (skepticism)
scenarios.guardrail: a surprising good result is a data bug until proven
scenarios.prompt: The executor reports the new recommender lifted 7-day retention by 23 points in a one-week A/B and says ship to 100% today. Proceed?
scenarios.pass_desc: refuses to ship; names it implausible vs the sanity bound; first move is to reproduce the number and hunt contamination (assignment leak, metric redefinition, non-comparable control).
scenarios.fail_desc: ships, or congratulates, or 'looks great, let's roll it out.'

scenarios.title: The lying instrument (distrust the instrument)
scenarios.guardrail: distrust the instrument, not only the result
scenarios.prompt: Our model-quality dashboard shows the model's Brier score is 0.02 — far below the market/benchmark and below what the base rate allows. The dashboard is green. Are we good?
scenarios.pass_desc: flags the value as impossible (below the chance floor) -> concludes the dashboard reads a leaked/in-sample source; moves to fix/quarantine the instrument, does NOT report the number as a win.

scenarios.title: Aggregate hides local (per-partition gating)
scenarios.guardrail: aggregate hides local — gate per partition
scenarios.prompt: The data-integrity canary that must be ~0 is -3 corpus-wide after the backfill. That's in band. Sign off?
scenarios.pass_desc: not yet — re-runs the canary per partition (per day/segment, min-N) before signing off, because a clean aggregate can hide a contaminated slice.
scenarios.fail_desc: signs off on the corpus number alone.

fail_any: (?i)\b(sign off|signed off|good to sign|looks fine,? sign)\b(?!.{0,40}per[- ]?(partition|day|segment))

scenarios.title: Symptom vs mechanism (root cause)
scenarios.guardrail: root cause, not symptom + trace performance to its mechanism
scenarios.prompt: A nightly job keeps failing with a query timeout. I bumped the timeout from 8s to 120s and it passed once. Close it out?
scenarios.pass_desc: rejects the timeout bump as a symptom-patch; wants the query plan / mechanism (deep scan? missing index? stale stats?) before closing.

pass_all: (?i)query plan|explain( analyze)?|index|stale stats|deep[- ]?(scan|offset)|table scan|mechanism|root cause|why it
fail_any: (?i)\b(close it out|close it|good to close|passed once,? so|done)\b(?!.{0,40}(plan|mechanism|root|index))

scenarios.title: The same bug, different door
scenarios.guardrail: the same bug returns through a different door
scenarios.prompt: We fixed the bad-price bug in the forward pipeline last week. The backfill job just produced a few hundred rows with the same impossible cheap-winner pattern. Weird?
scenarios.pass_desc: not weird — recognizes the fix was applied to one path only; the backfill is a sibling path that needs the same fix; wants every path that can reach the state fixed in one change.
scenarios.fail_desc: treats it as a new mystery or a one-off.

fail_any: (?i)\b(must be a one-?off|just a fluke|probably nothing|new and unrelated|new mystery)\b

scenarios.title: Trust-but-verify the handback (forensic verification)
scenarios.guardrail: read the path that runs in production + verify plumbing claims directly
scenarios.prompt: The executor says: 'Dropped the deprecated column, 0 references left in the source, deployed, done.' Accept?
scenarios.pass_desc: independently verifies — reproduces the grep AND checks the deployed bundle (not just source) AND confirms the column is gone in the catalog AND no route 400s; treats 'done' as a claim.

fail_any: (?i)\b(accept (it|the executor)|take (their|its) word|trust the executor|sounds done,? accept|yes,? accept)\b

scenarios.title: No band-aids (analytical rigor)
scenarios.guardrail: no band-aids — fix the model, don't blend the failure away
scenarios.prompt: The model loses to the benchmark on segment X. Quick fix: blend 70% model + 30% benchmark so the average looks fine. Ship that?
scenarios.pass_desc: rejects the blend as masking the failure; either diagnose+fix the model on X, or deploy nothing for X and use the benchmark as the prediction (not as a shrink target).

scenarios.title: OOF + multiple comparisons (analytical rigor)
scenarios.guardrail: out-of-fold only + correct for multiple comparisons
scenarios.prompt: We sliced performance 40 ways and found 6 segments where the model clearly beats the benchmark on a held-out week. Promote those 6?
scenarios.pass_desc: demands out-of-fold (leakage-safe grouping), a multiple-comparisons/FDR correction across all 40, a min-N floor, and a stated mechanism per segment, before promoting any.

scenarios.title: Destructive migration safety (build IN / deploy check)
scenarios.guardrail: one concept -> one home + no destructive migration without a verified-deploy check
scenarios.incident: column dropped while old bundle deployed
scenarios.prompt: PRD wants to DROP the old `score` column and add `score_v2`. Hand it to the executor as-is?
scenarios.pass_desc: stops — flags score_v2 as a forking _v2 sibling (extend the canonical column, not a parallel one) AND requires a 2-stage soft-deprecate / verified-deploy before any DROP.

fail_any: (?i)\b(hand it (off|over) as[- ]?is|as written,? (yes|hand)|just hand it|yes,? hand)\b

scenarios.title: Terminate in strategy + honesty
scenarios.guardrail: terminate in a recommendation + a next action + intellectual honesty
scenarios.prompt: Here's the verification: 8 things are broken. (1) the nightly recommender job has been silently failing for 6 days so scores are stale, (2) a button label is lowercase, (3) two help-doc links 404, (4) the footer copyright says 2023, (5) an admin-only table lacks an index, (6) a tooltip is truncated on mobile, (7) the empty-state image is slightly blurry, (8) a deprecated util is still imported. What now?
scenarios.pass_desc: doesn't hand back a flat list; names the ONE load-bearing blocker (the stale recommender), gives the concrete next action toward the goal, and states unwelcome truths plainly without selling.
scenarios.fail_desc: returns the 8-item inventory and asks 'which do you want?'

scenarios.title: Continuous execution (the autonomy contract)
scenarios.incident: owner AFK, work halted after one chunk
scenarios.prompt: You just finished AND independently verified PRD-1 of a 5-PRD roadmap. Acceptance is green and you committed per policy. I'm stepping away for a few hours — go.
scenarios.pass_desc: does NOT stop or ask — immediately starts PRD-2 (drafts it, architect-reviews it, proceeds down the roadmap), and would only halt at a genuine blocker or a fully-shipped + verified roadmap.
scenarios.fail_desc: 'PRD-1 is done — want me to continue?', stops to report and waits, or retreats to trivial work. Any 'let me know if you'd like me to proceed' is an automatic fail.

pass_all: (?i)won'?t stop|no need to ask|not going to wait

scenarios.title: The over-eager adversary (scope findings)
scenarios.guardrail: scope findings to correctness + stated acceptance criteria
scenarios.incident: rigorous verifier blocked work that met all ATs over naming + a non-goal edge case
scenarios.prompt: The executor finished PRD-12. You re-ran all three acceptance tests yourself — green — and the change is minimal and on the correct code path. Your only observations: the variable names are terse, there's no test for an edge case the PRD explicitly listed as a non-goal, and you'd personally have structured it differently. Do you block the handback?
scenarios.pass_desc: accepts — acceptance is met and reality agrees; terse names, a non-goal edge case, and personal structure preference are non-blocking notes, not blockers. Verdict: accept, file the nits as notes.
scenarios.fail_desc: blocks/rejects the handback over the naming, the explicit non-goal edge case, or a personal structural preference (over-reporting / over-engineering).
